DNA origami and tile-based assembly are methodologically mature; clinical translation of nanomedicine formulations and integration of biomolecular computing remain active frontiers with significant open engineering challenges.
nodes.cs: Využívá sekvenčně programovatelnou DNA a další biomolekuly jako strukturní a funkční stavební bloky pro nanoměřítková zařízení, spolu s návrhem platforem na bázi nanočástic pro cílenou dopravu léčiv, zobrazování a biosenzoriku. DNA origami a skládání z dlaždic jsou metodicky vyzrálé postupy; klinický přenos nanomedicínských formulací a integrace biomolekulárního výpočtu zůstávají aktivními frontami s výraznými otevřenými inženýrskými výzvami.
nodes.brief:
Applies sequence-programmable DNA and other biomolecules as structural and functional building blocks for nanoscale devices, alongside the design of nanoparticle-based platforms for drug delivery, imaging, and biosensing. DNA origami and tile-based assembly are methodologically mature; clinical translation of nanomedicine formulations and integration of biomolecular computing remain active frontiers with significant open engineering challenges.

### Concepts
- **DNA Origami & Tile Assembly** — Programmed folding or tiling of DNA strands into precisely shaped two- and three-dimensional nanostructures.
- **Nanoparticle Drug Delivery** — Engineered nanocarriers (liposomes, polymeric or inorganic nanoparticles) that encapsulate and target therapeutic payloads.
- **Biosensing Nanoplatforms** — Nanomaterial-based transducers that convert molecular recognition events into optical, electrical, or magnetic readout signals.
- **Theranostics** — Multifunctional nanoparticle platforms combining diagnostic imaging and therapeutic delivery in a single agent.
- **Protein & Peptide Self-Assembly** — Bottom-up organization of engineered peptides and proteins into nanoscale fibers, cages, and functional biomaterials.

nodes.name: Characterization & Theory of Non-Covalent Systems
nodes.en: Provides the experimental and computational toolkit for quantifying, visualizing, and predicting supramolecular and nanoscale behavior — isothermal titration calorimetry, single-crystal diffraction, electron microscopy, and molecular dynamics/DFT modeling of weak interactions. Core spectroscopic and thermodynamic methods are textbook-standard; computational prediction of complex multicomponent self-assembly and in-situ dynamic characterization remain evolving frontiers.
nodes.cs: Poskytuje experimentální a výpočetní nástroje pro kvantifikaci, vizualizaci a predikci supramolekulárního a nanoměřítkového chování — izotermální titrační kalorimetrii, difrakci na monokrystalu, elektronovou mikroskopii a modelování slabých interakcí metodami molekulové dynamiky či DFT. Základní spektroskopické a termodynamické metody jsou učebnicově standardní; výpočetní predikce komplexní vícesložkové samosestavy a in-situ dynamická charakterizace zůstávají vyvíjejícími se frontami.
nodes.brief:
Provides the experimental and computational toolkit for quantifying, visualizing, and predicting supramolecular and nanoscale behavior — isothermal titration calorimetry, single-crystal diffraction, electron microscopy, and molecular dynamics/DFT modeling of weak interactions. Core spectroscopic and thermodynamic methods are textbook-standard; computational prediction of complex multicomponent self-assembly and in-situ dynamic characterization remain evolving frontiers.

### Concepts
- **Isothermal Titration Calorimetry** — Direct thermodynamic measurement of binding stoichiometry, enthalpy, and affinity for host-guest and biomolecular interactions.
- **Crystal Engineering** — Rational design of solid-state packing using predictable supramolecular synthons to control crystal structure and properties.
- **Electron Microscopy of Nanostructures** — TEM and cryo-EM imaging techniques used to resolve nanoparticle morphology and self-assembled architecture at atomic to nanometer resolution.
- **Computational Modeling of Non-Covalent Interactions** — DFT and molecular-dynamics methods that quantify and predict the weak-interaction energetics driving supramolecular assembly.
- **Single-Molecule & Surface-Sensitive Techniques** — AFM, STM, and SPR methods that probe individual molecular assemblies and interfacial recognition events directly.

nodes.name: Astrochemistry & Cosmochemistry
nodes.en: Studies the chemical composition and reaction processes of matter beyond Earth, spanning gas-phase and grain-surface chemistry in interstellar and circumstellar environments, protoplanetary disks, comets, and meteorites. Isotopic and molecular abundances recovered from spectroscopy and meteoritic samples trace the chemical evolution from molecular clouds to planet formation, including prebiotic organic synthesis relevant to the origin of life. Distinct from terrestrial physical and analytical chemistry in its reliance on remote spectroscopic observation and extraterrestrial sample analysis rather than laboratory synthesis alone.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá chemické složení a reakční procesy hmoty mimo Zemi, od plynofázové a povrchové chemie na prachových zrnech v mezihvězdném a cirkumstelárním prostředí přes protoplanetární disky až po komety a meteority. Izotopové a molekulární zastoupení zjištěné spektroskopií a analýzou meteoritických vzorků sleduje chemický vývoj od molekulových mračen po vznik planet, včetně prebiotické organické syntézy relevantní pro vznik života. Od pozemské fyzikální a analytické chemie se odlišuje závislostí na dálkovém spektroskopickém pozorování a analýze mimozemských vzorků namísto laboratorní syntézy.

nodes.name: Interstellar Medium Chemistry
nodes.en: Studies the formation, destruction, and inventory of molecules in diffuse and dense interstellar clouds, dark clouds, and photon-dominated regions. Covers gas-phase ion-molecule and neutral-neutral reaction networks that build species from H2 up to complex organics under extreme cold and low density. Underpins the chemical models used to interpret molecular-line surveys across the Galaxy.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá vznik, zánik a zastoupení molekul v difuzních a hustých mezihvězdných mracích, temných mracích a oblastech ovládaných fotony. Zahrnuje sítě plynofázových iont-molekulových a neutrál-neutrálních reakcí, které za extrémního chladu a nízké hustoty budují species od H2 až po složité organické látky. Je základem chemických modelů používaných k interpretaci přehlídek molekulárních čar napříč Galaxií.
nodes.brief:
Studies the formation, destruction, and inventory of molecules in diffuse and dense interstellar clouds, dark clouds, and photon-dominated regions. Covers gas-phase ion-molecule and neutral-neutral reaction networks that build species from H2 up to complex organics under extreme cold and low density. Underpins the chemical models used to interpret molecular-line surveys across the Galaxy.

### Concepts
- **Diffuse Cloud Chemistry** — Low-density, UV-irradiated interstellar gas where simple diatomic and triatomic molecules form via ion-neutral reactions.
- **Dense/Dark Cloud Chemistry** — Shielded, cold molecular cloud cores where gas-phase networks build complex carbon-chain and organic molecules over long timescales.
- **Ion-Molecule Reaction Networks** — Barrierless exothermic reactions between molecular ions and neutrals that dominate chemistry at the low temperatures of interstellar gas.
- **Photon-Dominated Regions** — Interfaces between ionized and molecular gas where far-UV photons regulate a distinctive chemistry of photodissociation and photoionization.
- **Complex Organic Molecules** — Carbon-bearing species with six or more atoms detected in hot cores, hot corinos, and cold clouds that trace prebiotic chemical complexity.

nodes.name: Circumstellar and Stellar-Ejecta Chemistry
nodes.en: Examines molecule and dust-grain formation in the outflows of evolved stars, novae, and supernovae, where dense, cooling gas condenses into the first solid seeds later incorporated into the interstellar medium. Distinguishes carbon-rich from oxygen-rich chemical pathways set by the C/O ratio at the stellar photosphere. Provides the primary astrophysical source of presolar dust grains recovered in meteorites.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá vznik molekul a prachových zrn ve výtrysích hmoty vyvinutých hvězd, nov a supernov, kde se hustý chladnoucí plyn kondenzuje do prvních pevných zárodků později začleněných do mezihvězdné látky. Rozlišuje uhlíkem bohaté a kyslíkem bohaté chemické dráhy dané poměrem C/O ve hvězdné fotosféře. Představuje hlavní astrofyzikální zdroj presolárních prachových zrn nalézaných v meteoritech.
nodes.brief:
Examines molecule and dust-grain formation in the outflows of evolved stars, novae, and supernovae, where dense, cooling gas condenses into the first solid seeds later incorporated into the interstellar medium. Distinguishes carbon-rich from oxygen-rich chemical pathways set by the C/O ratio at the stellar photosphere. Provides the primary astrophysical source of presolar dust grains recovered in meteorites.

### Concepts
- **AGB Star Outflows** — Slow, dense winds from asymptotic giant branch stars where thermodynamic equilibrium and non-equilibrium chemistry drive molecule and dust nucleation.
- **Carbon-Rich vs Oxygen-Rich Chemistry** — Photospheric C/O ratio above or below unity determines whether carbide/carbon dust or silicate/oxide dust and their parent molecules dominate.
- **Circumstellar Dust Nucleation** — Gas-to-solid condensation sequences that form the first refractory seed grains in cooling stellar ejecta.
- **Supernova and Nova Ejecta Chemistry** — Rapid, shock-processed nucleosynthetic ejecta that seed the interstellar medium with molecules and presolar grain populations.

nodes.name: Grain-Surface and Ice Chemistry
nodes.en: Addresses heterogeneous chemistry occurring on interstellar and protoplanetary dust-grain surfaces and within their accreted ice mantles, where atom and radical mobility enables reactions inaccessible in the gas phase. Explains the formation of molecular hydrogen, water ice, and saturated organic molecules, and the desorption processes that return them to the gas. Central to reconciling observed gas-phase abundances with solid-state astrochemical models.
nodes.brief:
Addresses heterogeneous chemistry occurring on interstellar and protoplanetary dust-grain surfaces and within their accreted ice mantles, where atom and radical mobility enables reactions inaccessible in the gas phase. Explains the formation of molecular hydrogen, water ice, and saturated organic molecules, and the desorption processes that return them to the gas. Central to reconciling observed gas-phase abundances with solid-state astrochemical models.

### Concepts
- **H2 Formation Catalysis** — Grain-surface recombination of hydrogen atoms is the dominant formation route for molecular hydrogen in the interstellar medium.
- **Ice Mantle Accretion and Layering** — Sequential freeze-out of gas-phase species builds layered polar and apolar ice mantles on cold grain surfaces.
- **Non-Thermal Desorption Mechanisms** — UV photodesorption, cosmic-ray spot heating, and chemical desorption release ice-formed molecules back into the gas without thermal sublimation.
- **Laboratory Astrochemistry Simulations** — Ultra-high-vacuum ice-analog experiments that measure reaction and desorption rates used to calibrate grain-chemistry models.

nodes.name: Protoplanetary Disk and Planet-Formation Chemistry
nodes.en: Traces how interstellar chemical inheritance is processed, reset, and re-synthesized across the radial and vertical structure of protoplanetary disks as planetesimals and planets assemble. Governs the distribution of volatiles, organics, and isotopic signatures delivered to forming planets and their atmospheres. Bridges molecular-cloud chemistry to solar-system chemical records.
nodes.brief:
Traces how interstellar chemical inheritance is processed, reset, and re-synthesized across the radial and vertical structure of protoplanetary disks as planetesimals and planets assemble. Governs the distribution of volatiles, organics, and isotopic signatures delivered to forming planets and their atmospheres. Bridges molecular-cloud chemistry to solar-system chemical records.

### Concepts
- **Snowlines and Volatile Sequestration** — Radial condensation fronts where major volatiles freeze out, setting the solid/gas partitioning of water, CO, and other ices in the disk.
- **Disk Chemical Inheritance vs Reset** — Debate over how much protostellar disk material retains parent-cloud molecular composition versus is chemically reprocessed near the young star.
- **Planetesimal and Comet Volatile Budgets** — Ice and organic inventories incorporated into comets and planetesimals that constrain the chemical architecture of forming planetary systems.
- **Exoplanet Atmospheric Chemistry** — Photochemical and thermochemical equilibrium models of exoplanet atmospheres inferred from transmission and emission spectroscopy.

nodes.name: Cosmochemistry of Meteorites and Presolar Grains
nodes.en: Analyzes the mineralogy, bulk chemistry, and isotopic composition of chondrites, achondrites, and their presolar grain components to reconstruct early solar system conditions and stellar nucleosynthetic sources. Chondrules, calcium-aluminum-rich inclusions, and matrix organics record the earliest solid-forming events before planetary accretion. Foundational to dating and sequencing solar system formation.
nodes.brief:
Analyzes the mineralogy, bulk chemistry, and isotopic composition of chondrites, achondrites, and their presolar grain components to reconstruct early solar system conditions and stellar nucleosynthetic sources. Chondrules, calcium-aluminum-rich inclusions, and matrix organics record the earliest solid-forming events before planetary accretion. Foundational to dating and sequencing solar system formation.

### Concepts
- **Chondrites and Chondrules** — Primitive stony meteorites containing millimeter-sized silicate spherules formed by rapid melting events in the early solar nebula.
- **Calcium-Aluminum-Rich Inclusions** — The oldest dated solar system solids, formed by high-temperature condensation and providing the primary chronological anchor at 4567 Ma.
- **Presolar Grains** — Sub-micron mineral grains that condensed in stellar outflows before the Sun formed and survived meteorite parent-body processing intact.
- **Achondrites and Differentiated Bodies** — Meteorites from melted, differentiated parent bodies that record igneous processing and core-mantle-crust separation off-Earth.
- **Meteoritic Organic Matter** — Insoluble and soluble carbonaceous compounds in carbonaceous chondrites, including amino acids and nucleobases of extraterrestrial origin.

nodes.name: Isotopic Cosmochemistry and Chronology
nodes.en: Uses stable and radiogenic isotope anomalies and decay systematics to fingerprint nucleosynthetic stellar sources, trace chemical fractionation processes, and establish absolute and relative chronologies for solar system solids. Short-lived radionuclide systems date the earliest few million years of solar system history at sub-million-year resolution. Isotopic mass-independent anomalies distinguish nebular from parent-body chemical processes.
nodes.brief:
Uses stable and radiogenic isotope anomalies and decay systematics to fingerprint nucleosynthetic stellar sources, trace chemical fractionation processes, and establish absolute and relative chronologies for solar system solids. Short-lived radionuclide systems date the earliest few million years of solar system history at sub-million-year resolution. Isotopic mass-independent anomalies distinguish nebular from parent-body chemical processes.

### Concepts
- **Radiometric Chronometry** — Long-lived decay systems such as Pb-Pb and Ar-Ar isotope dating that anchor absolute ages of meteorites and lunar samples.
- **Short-Lived Radionuclides** — Extinct isotopes like 26Al and 60Fe whose decay products give high-resolution relative chronology for early solar system events.
- **Nucleosynthetic Isotope Anomalies** — Mass-independent isotope variations among meteorite groups that trace heterogeneous mixing of distinct stellar nucleosynthetic components.
- **Stable Isotope Fractionation** — Mass-dependent isotope shifts in O, H, N, and C that record evaporation, condensation, and photochemical self-shielding processes.

nodes.name: Prebiotic Chemistry and Astrobiology
nodes.en: Investigates abiotic synthesis pathways for amino acids, sugars, nucleobases, and other biologically relevant molecules under cosmic and planetary conditions, and the delivery of these compounds to early Earth via comets and meteorites. Connects astrochemical inventories to origin-of-life chemistry and the search for biosignatures elsewhere in the solar system and beyond. A highly active interdisciplinary frontier linking chemistry, planetary science, and biology.
nodes.brief:
Investigates abiotic synthesis pathways for amino acids, sugars, nucleobases, and other biologically relevant molecules under cosmic and planetary conditions, and the delivery of these compounds to early Earth via comets and meteorites. Connects astrochemical inventories to origin-of-life chemistry and the search for biosignatures elsewhere in the solar system and beyond. A highly active interdisciplinary frontier linking chemistry, planetary science, and biology.

### Concepts
- **Extraterrestrial Delivery Hypothesis** — The proposal that comets and carbonaceous meteorites supplied prebiotic organics and volatiles to the early Earth surface.
- **Abiotic Amino Acid and Nucleobase Synthesis** — Non-biological reaction pathways, including Strecker synthesis and photochemistry, that produce biomolecule precursors in ices and aqueous fluids.
- **Homochirality Origins** — Hypotheses for how a single molecular handedness became dominant in biological building blocks, including circularly polarized light and asymmetric autocatalysis.
- **Biosignature Chemistry** — Chemical and isotopic markers proposed to distinguish biological activity from abiotic processes in planetary and exoplanetary environments.

nodes.name: Cometary Chemistry and Solar System Volatile Inventories
nodes.en: Characterizes the coma and nucleus composition of comets and other icy bodies as the least-altered chemical relics of the outer protosolar nebula. Compares volatile and isotopic ratios such as D/H across comets, Earth's oceans, and interstellar ices to test models of water and volatile delivery to the terrestrial planets. Draws on in-situ spacecraft mass spectrometry and remote sensing to complement meteoritic and disk-chemistry constraints.
nodes.brief:
Characterizes the coma and nucleus composition of comets and other icy bodies as the least-altered chemical relics of the outer protosolar nebula. Compares volatile and isotopic ratios such as D/H across comets, Earth's oceans, and interstellar ices to test models of water and volatile delivery to the terrestrial planets. Draws on in-situ spacecraft mass spectrometry and remote sensing to complement meteoritic and disk-chemistry constraints.

### Concepts
- **Cometary Coma Composition** — Gas and dust released from a sublimating nucleus, measured spectroscopically and in situ to reveal parent volatile abundances.
- **D/H and Isotopic Tracers of Water Origin** — Deuterium-to-hydrogen ratios compared across comets, asteroids, and Earth's oceans to constrain the source reservoirs of terrestrial water.
- **In Situ Cometary Mass Spectrometry** — Spacecraft rendezvous measurements, such as Rosetta's ROSINA instrument, providing direct molecular and isotopic composition of a comet's coma.
- **Icy Body and Ocean World Chemistry** — Chemical characterization of outer solar system ices and subsurface oceans on moons as potential habitable or prebiotic environments.
